text: When a person who has fallen overboard is being picked up by a rescue boat, how should the boat normally approach with the wind?

options.text: Just off the bow and the victim to leeward
With wind directly on the beam and victim to windward
At full speed from windward side

explanation: A rescue boat should approach a person in the water with the windward side providing a lee; the victim should be positioned to leeward to prevent the boat from drifting onto the casualty.
